funds_in_out_pattern: large lump sum received, immediately split into multiple sub-₹50K transfers to different accounts within the same hour
l3_investigation_notes: Classic structuring pattern. ₹49,500 is deliberately just below the ₹50,000 UPI per-transaction soft threshold. 11 transactions in 1 hour from same sender to 11 different receivers — aggregate exceeds ₹5L within the hour. Combined with high avg monthly value (₹12L), 9 linked accounts, and 3 prior flags, this strongly suggests deliberate structuring to avoid CTR filing thresholds. Applicable rule: RBI Master Direction on KYC 2016 — Section 35 (Suspicious Transaction Reporting); PMLA 2002 Section 3 — structuring as a predicate offence.

funds_in_out_pattern: ₹34L credit received in 3 tranches over 6 hours, ₹28L forwarded out within 90 minutes across 12 different accounts, net balance near zero
l3_investigation_notes: Textbook mule account behaviour. 34-day-old account with Aadhaar OTP KYC only (limited KYC — max balance ₹1L per RBI guidelines, already breached here). ₹34L inflow forwarded out within 90 minutes to 12 different accounts — net balance maintained near zero. 12 linked accounts strongly suggests coordinated mule network. Avg monthly value of ₹28L is implausible for an account this age with minimal KYC. Applicable rule: RBI Master Direction on KYC — Section 16 (Simplified KYC limits); FIU-IND STR obligation under PMLA Rule 7 — funds passing through account without retention is a primary mule indicator.

funds_in_out_pattern: account dormant 180 days, single large outward RTGS at 3AM IST, IP resolves to Nigeria, declared destination is UAE, receiving bank is Dubai
l3_investigation_notes: Three-way geographic mismatch: account registered Tamil Nadu, transaction IP resolves to Nigeria, declared destination is UAE, receiving bank is Dubai. 180-day dormancy followed by ₹21.5L single outward RTGS at 3AM is highly anomalous. Avg monthly value is ₹95K — this transaction is 22x the monthly average. Purpose code 'family_support' is implausible for ₹21.5L. Form 15CA not filed — mandatory for outward remittances above ₹5L. LRS utilisation: ₹52L YTD against ₹187L limit — permissible but combined with other signals requires verification. Applicable rules: FEMA 1999 Section 10 — outward remittance procedures; RBI LRS Master Direction — Form 15CA/CB requirement; PMLA — geographic inconsistency as STR trigger.

funds_in_out_pattern: account dormant 520 days, ₹42L credited in two tranches within 48 hours from unknown corporate entity, immediately distributed in sub-₹5L chunks to 9 different individuals
l3_investigation_notes: Ghost account reactivation pattern. Account dormant 520 days — well above the 12-month threshold that triggers RBI mandatory re-KYC (Master Direction on KYC Section 38 — periodic updation). ₹42L credited from unknown corporate entity is wholly inconsistent with declared occupation (daily wage worker, expected monthly income ~₹15-20K). Avg monthly value of ₹22K makes this credit 190x the monthly average. Immediate distribution in sub-₹5L chunks to 9 individuals is a layering signal. Account has 3 linked accounts not previously flagged — re-KYC may reveal changed circumstances. Applicable rules: RBI KYC Master Direction Section 38 — periodic updation mandatory post-12-month dormancy; PMLA Section 3 — layering; FIU-IND STR obligation triggered by dormancy reactivation with large credit.

funds_in_out_pattern: regular monthly salary credit followed by predictable bill payments and small personal transfers — consistent pattern for 6 years
l3_investigation_notes: No suspicious indicators. Account 6+ years old, full KYC, salaried occupation consistent with transaction volumes. ₹4,200 electricity bill payment to BESCOM is a utility receiver — no STR or CTR obligation. Single transaction in last hour, 3 in 24h — well within normal range. No cross-border, no dormancy, no flags, no watchlist hits. L3 should dismiss with low confidence score (<0.15) and no STR generation. Short-circuit candidate at L1 on repeat.
